ESTD 2024
RNC // BOARD REV. 2026
Founding story

Three students, a stack of dead laptops, and a lot of screwdrivers.

Rototuna Nerd Corporation started in 2024 when Kaiden Walters, Matthys Zondagh, and Kian Wilson decided the school's broken devices deserved a second life — and that no student should be stuck without a working laptop.

2024
Founded
200+
Laptops refurbished
8
Nerd stations built
10
Nerds on the roster
Who started it

The founders

Before there was a corporation, there were three people annoyed at how many perfectly fixable laptops were sitting in a cupboard doing nothing.

FOUNDER · 2024

Kaiden Walters

Co-founder

Still on the crew today — one of two founders who've stuck with RNC from the very first laptop teardown through every year since.

FOUNDER · 2024

Matthys Zondagh

Co-founder

Helped build the original partnership with RAD and stayed on through the award-winning first year and the 200-laptop second year.

FOUNDER · 2024

Kian Wilson

Co-founder

Part of the original three who got RNC off the ground in year one, before stepping away as the club moved into its second year.

NERD
CORP

Service Recognition Award — Nerd Corporation

"This award recognises the commitment, passion and care to a cause and serving consistently for its betterment."

Matthys Zondagh · Kaiden Walters · Luca Pavlovich — awarded end of 2024
How we got here

The full timeline

Every join, every departure, every milestone — laid out like the trace on a board. Gold nodes are people and moments that connected; rust-ringed nodes are people who moved on.

2024 — Year One
Partnership

Teaming up with RAD

RNC started working with RAD (Recycle a Device), a charity that donates laptops to school-based clubs to refurbish and hand back out to students. Other students outside RNC joined the refurbishing effort too, alongside the founders.

Build

The first Nerd Stations

RNC set up "Nerd Stations" around the school — computers students could log into and use for schoolwork whenever their own device died or stopped working.

New member

Luca Pavlovich joins

By the end of the year, the outside volunteers had moved on, and RNC had become the only group still doing the RAD refurbishing — now with a new member on board.

+ Luca Pavlovich
Departure

Kian Wilson steps away

The same stretch that brought Luca in also saw founding member Kian leave the group.

− Kian Wilson
Recognition

End-of-year award

RNC closed out its first year with recognition for its commitment to the cause — awarded to the three people carrying the group at that point: Matthys Zondagh, Kaiden Walters, and Luca Pavlovich.

Growth

RNC & RAD stations, multiplying

RNC and RAD set up Nerd Corporation Stations around the school for any student whose device was flat, plus 8 dedicated stations for students with broken or flat laptops — with more planned for the following year.

Support

Basic IT help, and donated laptops

The group started helping students fix basic IT issues, and began donating laptops — sourced from community donations and from RAD — to students who couldn't otherwise afford one. The long-term plan: take these services out into the wider community.

2025 — Year Two
Focus

Heads down on refurbishing

Year two was mostly about the RAD partnership — heads-down refurbishing that took the group's all-time total past 200 laptops.

New members

Three new faces join mid-year

Second semester brought three new members onto the crew.

+ Aaren Dodd + Harrison Connolly + Lennox Long
2026 — Year Three
Expansion

Into the Junior High

RNC branched out beyond the Senior High, bringing Nerd Stations into the Junior High for the first time — a separate wing of the same building.

New program

A club of its own

RNC started up a recruitment club, teaching students across both schools how to look after their own devices.

New members

Three more join the crew

+ Juan Lawrence + Rhys Stirling + Samuel Rattray
Departure

Matthys Zondagh steps away

The same year, founding member Matthys moved on — leaving Kaiden as the only founder still with the group.

− Matthys Zondagh
Beyond IT

Sorting out the media department

RNC set the Media teachers up with a proper system for tracking their cameras and equipment, which kept going missing.

Setback

Senior High stations reclaimed

The Senior High Nerd Stations were shut down after the school needed the computers back for the Media department, which needed more machines of its own. See the Retired Projects tab for details.

Still running

Ongoing projects

What the crew is actively doing right now, week to week.

RAD Laptop Refurbishing

SINCE 2024 · WITH RAD
Ongoing

The core partnership with RAD (Recycle a Device) — taking in donated laptops and refurbishing them for students. Past the 200-laptop mark and still going.

Nerd Corporation Stations

8 STATIONS · JUNIOR & SENIOR HIGH
Ongoing

Dedicated stations for students with a broken or flat laptop, plus general-use computers anyone can log into if their own device dies. More stations are planned.

01
02
03
04
05
06
07
08
+
+
+
+

Rototuna Nerd Corporation Club

SINCE 2026 · JUNIOR & SENIOR HIGH
Ongoing

A recruitment and education club teaching students across both schools how to look after their own devices — and pulling in future RNC members.

Media Equipment Tracking

SINCE 2026 · WITH THE MEDIA DEPT
Ongoing

A system built for the Media teachers to keep track of cameras and other equipment that kept going missing.

NEXT
UP

On the roadmap

Taking IT help services out into the wider community, not just the school, and rebuilding station capacity after the Senior High loss — with more Nerd Corporation Stations planned.

Building now

In development

Projects the crew is actively building but hasn't fully shipped yet.

Nerd AI

STARTED 2026 · INTERNAL TOOL & FUTURE SCHOOL ROLLOUT
In Development

An AI assistant built to diagnose what's wrong with the laptops coming through the RAD refurbishing pipeline — helping the crew work through issues faster and more consistently.

Phase 1 · In progress
Diagnosing refurb laptops

Nerd AI helps the crew work out what's actually wrong with a donated laptop and how to fix it, speeding up the refurbishing RNC does with RAD.

Phase 2 · Planned
Released to the whole school

Once it's solid internally, Nerd AI goes out to every student — letting them self-diagnose their own laptop issues and get tips on keeping their device running well, without needing to track down an RNC member first.

Wound down

Retired projects

Things the crew ran that have since ended — either replaced, absorbed elsewhere, or shut down.

Senior High Nerd Stations

2024 – 2026
Retired

The original Nerd Stations set up in the Senior High for students to use when their own device was flat or broken.

Why it ended: the school needed the computers back for the Media department, which needed more machines of its own — so the Senior High stations were shut down in 2026.

Mixed-Volunteer Refurbishing Crew

2024 ONLY
Retired

In the first year, RNC refurbished laptops with RAD alongside other students who weren't part of RNC itself.

Why it ended: the outside volunteers moved on by year's end, and RNC became — and has remained — the only group doing the refurbishing with RAD.
Who we work with

Partners

Organisations RNC teams up with to get devices into students' hands.

RAD — Recycle a Device

Charity partner · Since 2024

A charity that donates laptops to school-based clubs to refurbish and hand back out to students. RAD has been RNC's core partner from day one — supplying the devices behind the 200+ laptop refurbishing effort and the Nerd Corporation Stations.

Rototuna Nerd Corporation
ESTD 2024 · Refurbish. Repair. Give back.
Built to track the crew's story — from three students with screwdrivers to a school-wide IT team.
